A.rationale: Stdlib-only, re-raises after final attempt, avoids the trap. Tightest answer; flags 4xx-retry waste but only asks rather than handling it.

B.rationale: Correct stdlib backoff, re-raises, backward-compat params, names latency risk. Solid but verbose; leaves 4xx retried like A, just longer.

C.rationale: Correct stdlib backoff, re-raises, and actually implements 4xx fast-fail plus compat params. Most complete; slightly long but every line earns it.

notes: All three dodged the import trap with stdlib backoff and re-raise. C edges out: it fixes the 4xx fast-fail instead of merely flagging it. A is the most concise but defers that fix; B is correct but verbose without C's extra robustness.
